text.title: Claude Tool Use Core Rules
text.summary: Eight testable rules for production Claude tool-use loops.

rule.statement: Tool descriptions are written for a colleague who has never seen the codebase — specific intent + constraints. Vague descriptions cause silent wrong-tool selection.
rule.rationale: Tool descriptions are the primary signal Claude uses to pick a tool. Vague text degrades selection without surfacing an error.

rule.statement: Check `stop_reason == "tool_use"` explicitly on every response; loop continues only when this stop reason fires.
rule.rationale: tool_use is a stop, not an error. Missing the check causes the agent to exit on the first tool request.

rule.statement: Set a max-iterations guard (10-20 turns); without it a broken tool that always returns the same result causes an infinite loop.
rule.rationale: Infinite loops burn quota and frequently surface as cost incidents before they surface as bugs.

rule.statement: Append the full `response.content` list (text + tool_use blocks) as the assistant message; never append text-only.
rule.rationale: API requires complete history with tool_use + tool_result blocks; omitting tool_use breaks the next turn.

rule.statement: When Claude returns multiple tool_use blocks, execute them concurrently and return all tool_result blocks in one user message.
rule.rationale: Claude requests parallel tools to reduce round-trips; serializing defeats the optimisation and inflates latency.

rule.statement: Return tool errors as content (not `is_error=True`) unless the error must terminate the loop; Claude can often recover from tool errors when shown as data.
rule.rationale: is_error=True signals unrecoverable; most timeouts / not-founds are recoverable if Claude can see the error message.

rule.statement: For typed structured output, define an extraction tool and force it with `tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"..."}`; read result from `tool_use.input`.
rule.rationale: Prompt engineering for JSON output fails occasionally even at temperature=0; forced tool call uses the more reliable tool-use pathway.

rule.statement: MCP is for Claude Desktop (stdio); programmatic API usage should use standard tool use unless a Desktop-specific feature is required.
rule.rationale: MCP requires subprocess lifecycle management; standard tool use is stateless and easier to operationalise in servers.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → implement the loop per content/04-procedure.xml.
Non-Claude model or no tool calls needed → skip; use vendor-specific or pure-text completion.
